guardrail_evaluations.explanation: The 10% discount is well inside the 20% soft ceiling for mid-market — no yellow flag on headline discount. The AE has room to flex modestly if needed without tripping this warn.
At 10% discount, this deal is well below the 30% threshold that requires Finance sign-off. No approval gate triggered on discount alone.
No ramp schedule is present on this deal (ramp_schedule_json is null), so ramp length is treated as 0 months — well within the 3-month limit. No approval required on this axis.
Estimated gross margin of ~33.3% clears the absolute floor of 25%. The deal is structurally viable. Note this estimate rests on the working assumption that list price carries a 40% gross margin.

Estimated margin of ~33.3% falls below the 40% warn threshold, triggered by the 10% list discount. Additionally, three non-standard concessions (rollover credits, free sandbox tenants, quarterly true-up) may carry hidden cost — RevRec and Finance should pressure-test whether rollover credits or sandbox provisioning compress realized margin further before this deal closes.

alternative_structures.label: Structure A: 36-Month Commit with Reduced Discount
alternative_structures.description: Extend the term from 24 to 36 months and reduce the headline discount from 10% to 6%, pricing the deal at $188,000 ACV. Rollover credits and sandbox tenants remain included, preserving the customer's key asks while recovering margin on a longer revenue runway.
alternative_structures.rationale: Notion gets the enterprise consolidation and usage flexibility they want; Clay gets a longer lock-in and 300bps of margin recovery. Decision velocity risk is lower because the deal economics are simpler and Finance sign-off risk stays well clear of guardrails.

alternative_structures.label: Structure B: Usage Commit Floor + Overage Upside (No Rollover)
alternative_structures.description: Hold proposed price at $180,000 but remove the rollover credits clause — instead, offer a 10% banked credit against the following year's renewal (not within-year rollover) if Notion exceeds 1.5M API call commit. Any usage above 1.5M is billed at the $0.0008 overage rate. This trades a RevRec-complicating clause for a retention incentive and usage upside.
alternative_structures.rationale: Notion's lumpy usage concern is addressed through a renewal credit rather than within-year rollover, which simplifies ASC 606 revenue recognition materially. Overage rate preserves upside if Notion scales; the banked credit is a renewal hook. ACV is flat but deal quality improves significantly.

alternative_structures.label: Structure C: Professional Services Unbundle + Lower Seat Discount
alternative_structures.description: Reduce the enterprise seat/usage discount to 5% ($190,000 ACV) and introduce a $15,000 onboarding/migration professional services line item for consolidating the 14 self-serve seats and Team plan into the enterprise contract. Sandbox tenants and quarterly true-up remain; rollover credits are dropped or capped at 10% of monthly commit.
alternative_structures.rationale: PS revenue is typically at or above 40% margin and does not carry the same discount pressure as SaaS seats — unbundling migration support recaptures dollars at full margin while giving Notion a concrete deliverable (clean consolidation) they genuinely need. Combined ACV rises to $205,000 and the blended margin improves. The AE's soft-commit on rollover is gracefully replaced with a more structured, scoped offer.

pricing.reasoning_summary: At a 10% discount, the headline deal clears all hard guardrails and sits safely below the Finance sign-off threshold, but the estimated gross margin of ~33.3% triggers the 40% warn guardrail — driven by the list discount and compounded by three non-standard concessions (rollover credits, free sandbox tenants, quarterly true-up) whose realized cost has not been modeled. All margin figures rest on the working assumption that list price carries a 40% gross margin; the CFO should refine COGS, especially for sandbox tenant provisioning and any RevRec liability from within-year rollover credits, before treating 33.3% as the floor. The AE's soft-commit on rollover is the highest-risk element: it likely complicates ASC 606 recognition and should be traded for a structurally cleaner mechanism (renewal credit or capped bank) before DocuSign. LTV estimate assumes linear ramp to 1.5M unit commit, then 15% YoY usage growth over a 3-year horizon at the $0.0008 overage rate plus base ACV.

performance_obligations.name: Enterprise platform subscription
performance_obligations.description: Consolidated enterprise SaaS subscription replacing 14 Pro seats and Team plan, including 1.5M committed API calls per year.
performance_obligations.rationale: Customer receives and consumes benefit of access to the platform continuously over the term; capable of being distinct and separately identifiable per ASC 606-10-25-19.

performance_obligations.name: API usage above committed units (overage)
performance_obligations.description: Usage-based consumption above the 1.5M committed call threshold at $0.0008/call, trued up quarterly.
performance_obligations.rationale: Overage represents a distinct usage-based service; recognized as usage occurs under the variable consideration / series guidance (ASC 606-10-32-40 series exception).

performance_obligations.name: Sandbox tenant access (4 tenants)
performance_obligations.description: Four non-production sandbox environments bundled at no incremental fee.
performance_obligations.rationale: Sandbox access is capable of being distinct (sold separately in market) and is delivered ratably; SSP must be allocated from the bundle even though invoiced at zero.

performance_obligations.name: Rollover credits within contract year
performance_obligations.description: Unused committed API calls roll month-to-month within each contract year.
performance_obligations.rationale: Not a separate PO; it is a feature of the usage-commit PO that affects the pattern and timing of consumption and breakage estimation.

variable_consideration_flags.explanation: Lumpy usage and intra-year rollover make breakage hard to estimate; revenue from committed-but-unused calls must be deferred and recognized as usage occurs or as breakage becomes probable (ASC 606-10-55-46 to -48).
Overage at $0.0008/call is variable and tied to actual customer activity; recognize as the customer consumes (allocation exception under ASC 606-10-32-40).
Quarterly reconciliation can produce credits or invoices that change transaction price; estimate must be updated each reporting period and constrained to amounts highly probable not to reverse.

contract_modification_risk.explanation: No MFN, expansion price-lock, or termination-for-convenience clauses are present. Quarterly true-ups are mechanical reconciliations against existing contract terms, not modifications under ASC 606-10-25-10.

recognized_revenue_schedule.notes: Subscription + sandbox allocated SSP recognized ratably; overage recognized as usage occurs; unused-but-committed credits deferred until consumed or breakage probable.
Same pattern; rollover does not cross contract years so unused Year 1 credits expire at year-end and any breakage is recognized then.

red_flags.label: Rollover credits create breakage estimation burden
red_flags.explanation: Intra-year rollover combined with lumpy usage means revenue cannot simply be recognized straight-line on the committed portion — finance must track consumption monthly and estimate breakage.

red_flags.label: Free sandbox tenants require SSP allocation
red_flags.explanation: Although invoiced at $0, sandbox tenants are a distinct PO and require allocation of transaction price based on standalone selling price, reducing revenue allocated to the subscription line.

red_flags.label: Quarterly true-up requires period-end estimate refresh
red_flags.explanation: Each quarter, transaction price must be reassessed and any catch-up adjustments recorded; ensure billing and revenue systems are reconciled.

red_flags.label: Two-week start request
red_flags.explanation: Aggressive timeline may pressure the deal desk; ensure ASC 606 treatment of rollover and sandbox is documented before signature.

asc606.reasoning_summary: The deal contains three distinct POs (enterprise subscription, overage usage, and bundled sandbox tenants), with rollover credits and quarterly true-ups introducing meaningful variable consideration that requires breakage estimation and quarterly reassessment. Contract-modification risk is low given absence of MFN/TFC/expansion-lock clauses, but SSP allocation to the zero-priced sandbox and breakage tracking on rollover credits warrant accounting policy alignment before signature. This is an automated estimate; final determination requires CFO/auditor review.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Any unused API call credits roll over month-to-month within the contract year, allowing Notion to carry forward unused capacity from low-usage months into higher-usage months.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Month-to-month rollover fundamentally alters the revenue recognition profile: credits that were already booked as earned in Month 1 remain as a contingent liability through Month 12, preventing Clay from recognizing revenue ratably. At $180K ACV over 24 months, this creates a rolling deferred-revenue exposure of up to ~$15K/month per contract year, and sets a precedent that other PLG-converted customers will cite. The AE has already soft-committed, which increases concession pressure.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Unused API call credits shall not roll over on a monthly basis. In lieu of monthly rollover, Customer may carry forward up to fifteen percent (15%) of the annual committed credit volume (i.e., up to 225,000 credits per contract year) into the immediately following contract quarter, expiring at the end of such quarter, and in no event carrying over into a subsequent contract year. Credits do not carry over at contract renewal.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept rollover capped at 10% of annual committed volume (150,000 credits), usable only in the immediately following calendar quarter, with hard expiry at contract year-end. No cross-year carryover under any circumstance.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has accepted capped quarterly rollover (10–15% of annual commit) with a single-quarter expiry for one prior PLG-to-enterprise conversion; uncapped month-to-month rollover has not been accepted.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Billing is annual upfront against the committed usage volume, with a quarterly reconciliation of actual vs. committed usage — overages invoiced and under-usage credited each quarter.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Quarterly credits for under-usage effectively convert the annual upfront commit into a de facto quarterly pay-as-you-go arrangement. If Notion under-uses in Q1 and Q2 and receives credits, Clay has issued cash-equivalent concessions against already-recognized ARR, creating both a cash-flow and rev-rec complication. Overage invoicing quarterly is fine; credit issuance for under-usage is the exposure.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Customer shall be invoiced annually in advance at the committed usage volume. Quarterly true-up shall apply to overage only: actual usage in excess of the pro-rata quarterly allotment (375,000 credits per quarter) shall be invoiced at the overage rate ($0.0008/credit) within thirty (30) days of quarter-end. Under-usage in any quarter shall not result in a credit or offset; unused committed credits are subject to the rollover policy in Section [X] and do not reduce the annual commitment invoice.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept under-usage credits only at year-end (not quarterly), capped at 5% of annual ACV ($9,000), applied as a discount coupon against the renewal invoice — not as a cash credit.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Quarterly overage true-up is standard; quarterly under-usage crediting has not been extended to any mid-market customer and would be a new precedent.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Four (4) sandbox tenants are included at no additional charge as part of the enterprise plan.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Sandboxes consume real API capacity and infrastructure. Bundling four free tenants without a usage cap means Notion's RevOps or engineering teams could run non-trivial workloads through sandbox environments against the committed credit pool — or, if sandboxes are carved out from the pool, Clay eats that infrastructure cost. Risk is low if usage is capped but becomes medium if sandboxes share the production credit pool.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Customer shall receive two (2) sandbox tenant environments at no additional charge. Each sandbox tenant shall be capped at 50,000 API credits per contract year, drawn from the annual committed usage pool. Additional sandbox tenants beyond two (2) may be provisioned at 20% of the per-seat list rate per tenant per year. Sandbox tenants may not be used for production workloads.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept four (4) sandbox tenants, each capped at 25,000 credits per year drawn from the committed pool, with a contractual prohibition on production use and a right to audit.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Two free sandbox tenants with credit caps is the current standard enterprise entitlement; four free tenants without a usage cap has not been offered.

standard_clauses_affirmed: Payment terms: annual upfront billing — standard
Discount: 10% for multi-year consolidation — within AE authority
redline.one_line_summary: We can close this in two weeks, but we must cap the rollover at 15% / one quarter, strip the quarterly under-usage credit in favor of year-end-only, and limit free sandboxes to two capped tenants — otherwise we have a rev-rec problem and a precedent that will haunt every future PLG conversion.
redline.reasoning_summary: The headline risk is the combination of uncapped month-to-month credit rollover and quarterly under-usage crediting, which together transform a clean annual-upfront ARR deal into a contingent-liability arrangement that finance and rev-rec will flag. The AE's soft-commit on rollover increases urgency to land an explicit counter before the deal advances. Notion's strong preference for Clay over Clearbit (given existing self-serve footprint and the RevOps champion) gives Clay meaningful negotiating leverage to hold firm on the rollover cap and the quarterly credit strip. Sandbox risk is secondary and easily resolved with a two-tenant / credit-cap compromise.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Standard sub-$100K with ≤15% discount → AE Manager
required_approvers.rationale: Default approval gate: this rule fires for all deals and AE Manager is always the entry point.
Deal ACV of $180K falls within the $100K–$500K band, requiring RevOps review for structure consistency and usage-commit validation.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Non-standard payment terms route to Finance
required_approvers.rationale: Annual upfront billing with quarterly true-up (a non-standard cash-timing and reconciliation mechanism) requires Finance review for cash recognition and breakage estimation, particularly given the rollover-credits revenue complexity flagged by ASC 606 Agent.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Redline Agent flagged high-priority non-standard clauses (rollover credits, quarterly true-up, free sandboxes)
required_approvers.rationale: Three non-standard clauses present (rollover_credits_within_contract_year, quarterly_true_up, sandbox_tenants_included_free) with overall_redline_priority marked 'high'; Legal must scope and counter-propose to cap rollover, strip under-usage credits, and cap sandbox entitlements before execution.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: ASC 606 red-flag severity ≥ 'warn' + margin pressure from three non-standard concessions
required_approvers.rationale: ASC 606 Agent flagged two 'warn' red flags (rollover credits create breakage estimation burden; free sandbox tenants require SSP allocation) and Pricing Agent's 'warn' guardrail on gross margin (33.3% vs. 40% threshold) driven by list discount and concession cost. CFO must pressure-test realized margin, validate revenue recognition treatment, and sign off on the deal economics post-negotiation.

blockers_to_address_first: CRITICAL: AE must secure Notion's agreement to cap rollover credits at 15% of annual committed volume (225,000 credits) with hard expiry at end of following quarter — do not advance to Legal/Finance without this scope agreement in writing, as uncapped month-to-month rollover creates a 'high' rev-rec and cash-flow risk.

CRITICAL: AE must obtain Notion's written acceptance of year-end-only under-usage crediting (capped at 5% of ACV, applied as renewal discount, not cash credit) in place of quarterly under-usage credits — quarterly credits effectively convert annual upfront to pay-as-you-go and will trigger Finance/CFO veto.

CRITICAL: AE must confirm Notion will accept two (2) free sandbox tenants capped at 25,000 credits each per contract year, or negotiate up to four (4) tenants capped at 15,000 credits each — free, uncapped sandboxes introduce medium risk to usage-commit structure and create bad precedent.

Finance and CFO must complete ASC 606 breakage-estimation model for rollover credits before signature — revenue deferral schedule and quarterly catch-up process must be documented and aligned with Accounting.

Legal must prepare final redline countering all three non-standard clauses with the fallback positions (cap rollover ≤15%, year-end credits only, sandbox caps) and obtain Notion's signature before deal moves to CFO review.
approval.one_line_summary: Deal routes AE Manager → RevOps + Finance + Legal (parallel) → CFO. Expect ~7 business days. Non-negotiable blockers: cap rollover at 15% with hard expiry, strip quarterly under-usage credits in favor of year-end only, limit free sandboxes to 2–4 capped tenants — or Finance and CFO will veto due to rev-rec complexity and margin exposure. Close timeline (two weeks) is feasible only if AE lands these three concession bounds with Notion immediately.
approval.reasoning_summary: This is a mid-market expansion deal with a clean 10% headline discount and no MFN/compliance clauses, but the three non-standard concessions (rollover credits, quarterly true-up, free sandboxes) combine to create a 'high' redline priority and two ASC 606 'warn' flags that escalate approval from a simple RevOps + AE Manager review to a full Finance + Legal + CFO chain. The deal's profitability (33.3% margin vs. 40% guardrail warn) is already under pressure from the list discount; the soft-committed rollover and quarterly under-usage credits add hidden cost and revenue deferral risk that Finance and CFO must resolve before execution. RevOps, Finance, and Legal can run in parallel (step 2) because the blockers are blockers — the AE must negotiate Notion's acceptance of the cap-rollover, year-end-credits, and sandbox-cap positions in writing before any downstream approver can sign off. CFO is the final step (step 3) to validate margin and revenue recognition post-negotiation. The two-week close timeline is achievable if the AE moves on the blockers immediately; otherwise the deal will slip into mid-to-late June.

text.text: Deal Review: Notion — Self-Serve to Enterprise Conversion

fields.text:
*Approval Chain*
AE Manager → RevOps + Finance + Legal (parallel) → CFO

text.text:
*What the deal team needs to know:*

• *Rollover credits are a rev-rec blocker.* The AE has soft-committed to uncapped month-to-month rollover — this must be countered immediately. Finance and CFO will veto without a written cap (≤15% of annual commit, expiring end of following quarter). Do not advance to Legal/Finance review until Notion accepts the cap in writing.

• *Quarterly under-usage credits must be stripped.* As structured, quarterly credits for under-usage convert an annual-upfront ARR deal into a de facto quarterly arrangement — a new precedent that triggers ASC 606 breakage estimation complexity and a Finance/CFO veto. Counter: year-end-only credits, capped at 5% of ACV, applied as a renewal discount only.

• *Margin is already under the 40% warn threshold and the three non-standard concessions (rollover, true-up, free sandboxes) add unmodeled cost.* CFO must pressure-test realized margin and validate ASC 606 treatment — including SSP allocation for the zero-priced sandbox tenants — before signature. The two-week customer timeline is achievable only if the AE locks in all three concession bounds with Notion today.

elements.text: *AE Owner:* Devon Wright (mgr: Sarah Goldstein)  |  review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

slack_post.plaintext_fallback:
DEAL REVIEW: Notion — Self-Serve to Enterprise Conversion | ACV: $180K | TCV: $360K (24-mo) | Discount: 10% | Est. Margin: ~33.3% (below 40% warn threshold) | Approval: AE Manager → RevOps + Finance + Legal (parallel) → CFO (~7 business days).
CRITICAL: AE has soft-committed to uncapped month-to-month rollover credits — must be capped at 15% of annual commit with hard quarterly expiry before deal advances; Finance/CFO will veto otherwise.
CRITICAL: Quarterly under-usage credits must be replaced with year-end-only crediting (5% of ACV cap, renewal discount only) — current structure converts annual upfront to pay-as-you-go and creates ASC 606 complexity.
Margin is already below the 40% guardrail warn; CFO must validate realized margin and revenue recognition (incl. SSP allocation for free sandbox tenants) before signature. Two-week close is feasible only if AE lands all three concession bounds with Notion immediately.
AE: Devon Wright | Review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

ae_email_draft.subject: [Notion] Deal desk complete — 3 blockers you must resolve before this goes to approvals
ae_email_draft.body_markdown:
Devon, pricing clears all hard guardrails at 10% and the deal is structurally solid, but three AE-owned blockers must be resolved in writing with Notion **before** this enters the approval chain — without them, Finance and CFO will veto.

1. **Cap rollover credits:** Counter Notion's uncapped month-to-month rollover with a 15% annual commit cap (225K credits) carrying into the next quarter only, expiring hard at contract year-end. Your soft-commit needs to be walked back now — uncapped rollover is a rev-rec blocker under ASC 606.
2. **Strip quarterly under-usage credits:** Overage true-ups quarterly are fine; under-usage credits are not. Counter with year-end-only credit, capped at 5% of ACV ($9K), applied as a renewal discount — not cash. Quarterly under-usage credits convert annual upfront ARR into pay-as-you-go and will trigger a Finance veto.
3. **Limit free sandboxes:** Notion asked for 4 free, uncapped tenants. Counter with 2 free tenants capped at 25K credits/year each (fallback: 4 tenants at 15K credits each) — uncapped sandbox access creates usage-commit leakage and a bad precedent for every future PLG conversion.

Once Notion agrees to these three positions in writing, route to Sarah (AE Manager) first, then RevOps + Finance + Legal run in parallel, and CFO is the final gate. Budget **~7 business days** for the full chain — your May 18 close is still achievable if you get Notion's written acceptance on all three points today.

customer_email_draft.subject: Notion Enterprise Consolidation — Our Position on the Three Open Items
customer_email_draft.body_markdown:
Thanks for moving quickly on the consolidation — your team's self-serve history with Clay makes this a natural fit, and we want to get you live in two weeks if the structure works for both sides.

On **rollover credits**: we can't accept uncapped month-to-month rollover — it creates a revenue recognition problem on our end that Finance and the CFO will not approve. What we can offer is a carry-forward of up to 15% of your annual committed volume (225,000 credits) into the immediately following quarter, with a hard expiry at that quarter's end and no cross-year carryover. That addresses your lumpy-usage concern without the accounting liability.

On **quarterly true-up**: quarterly overage invoicing is fully standard and stays as proposed. The piece we need to remove is the quarterly under-usage credit — issuing intra-year credits against an annual upfront commit effectively converts it to pay-as-you-go, which neither our rev-rec nor our cash-flow model supports. We can offer a year-end reconciliation instead: if you finish the year below commit, we'll apply up to 5% of ACV ($9,000) as a discount coupon toward your Year 2 renewal invoice, not a cash credit.

On **sandbox tenants**: two tenants with a per-tenant credit cap is our standard enterprise entitlement. We can stretch to four tenants if each is capped at 25,000 API credits per year drawn from your committed pool, with a contractual prohibition on production use — that gets you the environments you need for testing without exposing the usage-commit structure.

The headline price ($180,000 ACV, 10% multi-year discount, annual upfront, 24-month term) is confirmed and doesn't move with these changes. If you can confirm in writing that the three positions above are acceptable in principle, our Legal and Finance teams can turn a clean redline within the approval window — the two-week start date stays in reach, but we need that confirmation this week to keep the internal chain moving.

sections.heading: Headline
sections.content_markdown: Notion's RevOps team is consolidating 14 Pro seats + a Team plan into a single 24-month enterprise contract at **$180K ACV / $360K TCV**, billed annually upfront. Three non-standard concessions requested by the customer materially change the rev-rec and cash-flow profile and must be renegotiated before signature.

sections.heading: Pricing Summary
sections.content_markdown:
| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| List Price | $200,000 ACV |
| Proposed Price | $180,000 ACV |
| Effective Discount | **10%** (within AE authority; soft ceiling is 20%) |
| Estimated Gross Margin | **~33.3%** ⚠️ below 40% warn threshold |
| Usage Commit | 1.5M API calls/yr; overage at $0.0008/call |
| LTV Estimate | ~$1.19M (3-yr, 15% usage growth assumption) |

**Margin pressure drivers:** headline discount + unmodeled cost of rollover credits, free sandbox tenants, and quarterly true-up. Finance/CFO must validate realized margin before close.

**Best alternative – Structure B (recommended if rollover must stay):** Hold $180K ACV but replace within-year rollover with a 10% banked renewal credit; eliminates the ASC 606 breakage problem with no ACV impact. Structure A (36-month at $188K, 6% discount) recovers ~300bps of margin if Notion will extend term.

sections.heading: Risk Findings
sections.content_markdown:
**🔴 HIGH – Rollover credits (Redline + ASC 606)**
- AE has *soft-committed* to uncapped month-to-month rollover — this has **never been accepted** by Clay.
- Creates up to ~$15K/month rolling deferred-revenue exposure; prevents ratable revenue recognition.
- ASC 606 requires breakage estimation (high difficulty) on unused committed credits each period.
- **Required action:** Cap rollover at 15% of annual commit (225K credits), hard expiry end of following quarter. Do not advance to Legal/Finance without written scope agreement from Notion.

**🔴 HIGH – Quarterly under-usage credits (Redline)**
- Quarterly crediting for under-usage converts annual upfront ARR into de facto pay-as-you-go.
- No mid-market precedent; Finance/CFO veto risk is high.
- **Required action:** Strip quarterly under-usage credits; replace with year-end-only credit capped at 5% of ACV ($9K), applied as renewal discount (not cash).

**🟡 MEDIUM – Free sandbox tenants (Redline + ASC 606)**
- 4 free, uncapped sandboxes are above Clay's standard 2-tenant entitlement and require SSP allocation as a distinct performance obligation under ASC 606.
- Infrastructure cost unmodeled; sets bad precedent for PLG conversions.
- **Required action:** Accept max 4 tenants capped at 15K–25K credits each/year drawn from committed pool, with production-use prohibition.

**🟡 MEDIUM – Margin guardrail warn**
- Estimated 33.3% gross margin is below the 40% warn threshold. Hidden costs from the three concessions may compress this further.

**ℹ️ INFO – Two-week start request**
- Aggressive but feasible *only* if AE resolves the three blockers immediately. Otherwise deal slips to late June.

sections.heading: Approval Routing
sections.content_markdown:
| Step | Approver | Veto? |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | AE Manager (Sarah Goldstein) | No |
| 2 (parallel) | RevOps | No |
| 2 (parallel) | Finance | **Yes** |
| 2 (parallel) | Legal | **Yes** |
| 3 | CFO | **Yes** |

**Estimated cycle time: ~7 business days** (feasible for May 18 close *only* if AE lands written concession bounds with Notion by EOD May 6).

**Pre-routing blockers (AE must clear first):**
1. Written agreement from Notion on capped rollover (≤15%, one-quarter expiry)
2. Written agreement on year-end-only under-usage credit (not quarterly cash)
3. Written agreement on sandbox caps (2–4 tenants, credit-capped, no production use)

sections.heading: Recommendation
sections.content_markdown:
**🟡 Ship-with-conditions**

The headline discount and term structure are clean and approvable, but the deal must not advance to DocuSign until the AE replaces the soft-committed uncapped rollover and quarterly under-usage credits with the capped/year-end structures above — failure to do so will trigger Finance and CFO vetoes and create lasting rev-rec and PLG-conversion precedent risk.

comms.reasoning_summary: Calibrated customer email to collaborative tone given the deal context. AE email leads with verdict (ship-with-conditions (blockers must clear first)) and a numbered next-step list. One-pager and Slack post both headline the recommendation and surface 3 flagged redlines (3 countered in customer reply). Approval routing summary: ~7 business days through 5-step chain.

synthesis: Pricing clears hard guardrails at a 10% discount but trips the 40% margin warn at ~33.3%, with three unmodeled concessions (rollover credits, free sandboxes, quarterly true-up) likely pushing realized margin lower. ASC 606 flags four issues across three POs, chiefly variable-consideration and breakage exposure from rollover credits and quarterly true-ups, plus SSP allocation to the zero-priced sandbox. The top redline risk is the uncapped month-to-month rollover combined with quarterly under-usage crediting, which converts a clean annual-upfront ARR into a contingent liability — and Clay has the leverage (Notion prefers us over Clearbit) to hold firm on a cap and strip. Approval escalates to a full AE Mgr → RevOps → Finance → Legal → CFO chain (~7 business days), with RevOps/Finance/Legal running in parallel only after the AE secures written concessions on the three blockers. Recommendation: ship-with-conditions in a collaborative tone, but the blockers must clear in writing before downstream approvers engage or the two-week close slips to mid-to-late June.

signals.summary: Notion is pursuing a major strategic shift led by CEO Ivan Zhao. The key takeaways: - New product focus: Notion will publicly launch custom agents capable of answering repetitive questions, triaging tickets, and performing cross-organizati…
